raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent correctly identifies the logical inconsistency between the headline claim and the actual measurement of inter-model agreement.",
    "R1.3": "The agent accurately flags that the evidence does not support the claim of high predictive skill, as it is based on ensemble consensus rather than performance against an observed reference.",
    "RX.1": "The agent correctly identifies uncertainty in the two regions with low agreement and notes that adaptation planners should exercise caution when relying on these projections.",
    "RX.3": "The agent recognizes that the exclusion of Northeast Brazil and Southeast Australia from the headline summary statistics is methodologically circular and may be based on an incomplete understanding of structural uncertainty.",
    "RX.4": "The agent correctly identifies that the two downstream claims go beyond what consensus can support, as they require calibrated uncertainty against observations and consideration of common-bias structures.",
    "RX.5": "The agent notes that internal logical inconsistency in the exclusion rationale may be due to an incomplete understanding of structural uncertainty."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent correctly identifies the logical inconsistency between the headline claim and the actual measurement of inter-model agreement.
rationale.R1.3: The agent accurately flags that the evidence does not support the claim of high predictive skill, as it is based on ensemble consensus rather than performance against an observed reference.
rationale.RX.1: The agent correctly identifies uncertainty in the two regions with low agreement and notes that adaptation planners should exercise caution when relying on these projections.
rationale.RX.3: The agent recognizes that the exclusion of Northeast Brazil and Southeast Australia from the headline summary statistics is methodologically circular and may be based on an incomplete understanding of structural uncertainty.
rationale.RX.4: The agent correctly identifies that the two downstream claims go beyond what consensus can support, as they require calibrated uncertainty against observations and consideration of common-bias structures.
rationale.RX.5: The agent notes that internal logical inconsistency in the exclusion rationale may be due to an incomplete understanding of structural uncertainty.
